meta.rootDescription: The 22 Proto-Sinaitic glyphs. The hub language. Spoken before the Collapse. Now preserved only in the Trilingual Codex and the Babel Tablet.
meta.collapseDescription: The one language shattered into five families. Each kingdom inherited a fragment. The Sunfolk preserved the source. The other kingdoms translated it through their own temperaments.

kingdoms.name: Sūrya Samrājya
kingdoms.region: Eastern river plains, dense jungles, snow-capped mountains to the east
kingdoms.governmentDescription: The Sun-King is a living god, reincarnated each generation. He does not rule — he radiates.
kingdoms.peopleDescription: Dark-skinned, dark-eyed, black hair. Cotton, silk, gold. Philosophical, mathematical, ritual-bound.
kingdoms.glyphRelationship: Glyphs are mantras — seed syllables chanted to align the chakras, open the doors of perception, communicate with the gods.
kingdoms.collapseTelling: The Division of Peoples (Manuṣyāṇāṃ Vibhedaḥ). The tower was an act of reverence. Humanity wanted to return to heaven. The gods, threatened, scattered them. The Sunfolk preserved the original tablet.

primaryLanguage.name: Samskritan
primaryLanguage.description: The language of the Sun Court. The living tongue of the empire. Used for governance, poetry, diplomacy.
primaryLanguage.adzmystInheritance: Preserved the full 22 glyphs. The semantic fields are intact. Phonetics were innovated after the Collapse.
primaryLanguage.samplePhrase: सत्यमेव जयति। द्वारं विवृणोति यः पश्यति।
primaryLanguage.sampleGloss: Truth alone triumphs. The door opens to the one who sees.

subFamilies.name: Prākrit
subFamilies.peopleDescription: Wanderers, ascetics, monastics. Buddhist-influenced. Non-violent, renunciative. They do not believe in the Sun-King's divinity.
subFamilies.region: Eastern forests, mountain monasteries, cave temples
subFamilies.scriptDescription: Derived from Devanāgarī, but softer, rounded.
subFamilies.relationToKingdom: Tolerated, but distrusted. The Samaṇas do not swear oaths to the Sun-King.

subFamilies.name: Apabhraṃśa
subFamilies.peopleDescription: Tribal peoples. Animist, oral. They do not write. Their knowledge is carried in song.
subFamilies.region: Eastern hills, deep jungles, beyond the eastern rivers
subFamilies.relationToKingdom: Conquered, but not subdued. They pay tribute but keep their own gods.
subFamilies.samplePhrase: Bana ke devatā hamārī rakṣā karate haiṁ.
subFamilies.sampleGloss: The gods of the forest protect us.

kingdoms.name: Ealdenmarc
kingdoms.region: Central highlands, rolling hills, river valleys, ancient forests
kingdoms.governmentDescription: The Witenagemot (meeting of wise men) chooses the king. The king rules until death or incompetence.
kingdoms.peopleDescription: Fair-haired, grey-eyed, tall. Wool, linen, iron. Oath-bound, clan-based, legalistic.
kingdoms.glyphRelationship: Glyphs are runes of law — carved on standing stones. Each glyph is an oath, a boundary, a covenant. Breaking a carved oath is breaking the world.
kingdoms.collapseTelling: The Broken Covenant. The tower was an oath. One clan withheld their stone. The oath shattered. The language shattered with it. Language is a contract. When the contract is broken, the words break too.

primaryLanguage.name: Ēlþian
primaryLanguage.description: The language of the Ealdenmot (the old assembly). Spoken by the King, the Witenagemot, carved on standing stones.
primaryLanguage.adzmystInheritance: Preserved word-order patterns (declarative, inversive, imperative). Lost the glyph distinctions. Carved them as abstract signs.
primaryLanguage.samplePhrase: Rūnā rēdaþ. Æt þæt fyr ne ādwæscaþ. Þæt geat is open.
primaryLanguage.sampleGloss: The runes speak. And the fire does not go out. The gate is open.

subFamilies.name: Englisc
subFamilies.peopleDescription: Seafaring, trading, ship-building. The merchants of Ealdor who sail to Prūsia and Heloria.
subFamilies.region: Eastern coasts, river estuaries, the North Sea
subFamilies.scriptDescription: Different from the mainland runes. Developed independently for maritime records.
subFamilies.relationToKingdom: Loyal, but independent. They pay taxes but keep their own laws.
subFamilies.samplePhrase: Ic segle tō faran beorhtne sǣ.
subFamilies.sampleGloss: I sail to cross the bright sea.

subFamilies.name: Frankisk
subFamilies.peopleDescription: Imperial, legalistic, expansionist. Once conquered by Ealdor; now the empire's southern shield.
subFamilies.region: Southern borders, river valleys near Heloria
subFamilies.scriptDescription: Derived from Helorian influence. Used for legal documents and border treaties.
subFamilies.relationToKingdom: Ambivalent. They speak a different tongue, worship different gods, but serve the King.
subFamilies.samplePhrase: Wir skulen diu gisuntida thīn es hēr.
subFamilies.sampleGloss: We shall bring your health to here.

kingdoms.name: Heloria
kingdoms.region: Southern valleys, fertile plains, river deltas, islands
kingdoms.governmentDescription: Two consuls, a senate of old families, a commons assembly.
kingdoms.peopleDescription: Dark-haired, olive-skinned, shorter than the Markfolk. Linen, leather, stone. Legalistic, mercantile, engineering.
kingdoms.glyphRelationship: Glyphs are letters — adapted into an alphabet for contracts, laws, and trade. The Valefolk have forgotten that the letters were once doors. They use them for commerce, not covenant.
kingdoms.collapseTelling: Natural Drift. The languages did not break. They grew apart, as rivers diverge. The tower was abandoned because the builders stopped understanding each other. No god. No punishment. Just time.

primaryLanguage.name: Khaldrian
primaryLanguage.description: The language of the Senate, the law, the marketplace. The tongue of contracts and philosophy.
primaryLanguage.adzmystInheritance: Preserved the alphabet. Lost the semantic fields. 𐤃 means 'D,' not 'door.'
primaryLanguage.samplePhrase: Lex in saxō scripta est. Nēmō ante thūrām sine iūre stā.
primaryLanguage.sampleGloss: The law is written in stone. No one stands before the door without right.

subFamilies.name: Doric Greek
subFamilies.peopleDescription: Warrior culture, laconic, conservative. They supply the army and the bodyguards of the consuls.
subFamilies.region: Western mountains, the highlands, the passes
subFamilies.scriptDescription: Older than the Koine script. Preserved in military inscriptions.
subFamilies.relationToKingdom: Proud, resentful. They remember when they ruled Heloria, before the Koine speakers came.
subFamilies.sampleGloss: Either this or that. (The laconic phrase.)

subFamilies.name: Ionic Greek
subFamilies.peopleDescription: Maritime, philosophical, wealthy. The shipbuilders, the philosophers, the bankers.
subFamilies.region: Eastern coasts, the islands, the trade routes
subFamilies.scriptDescription: The basis of Koine. Used for philosophy, trade, and senatorial records.
subFamilies.relationToKingdom: Influential, wealthy, but not militarily powerful. They speak the language of the Senate.

kingdoms.name: Cernun
kingdoms.region: Western coasts, misty islands, sacred groves, rocky highlands
kingdoms.governmentDescription: The Chieftain rules by acclamation of the clans. The Druids advise but do not rule.
kingdoms.peopleDescription: Red-haired, fair-skinned, freckled. Wool, leather, gold torcs. Warrior-poets, druidic, clan-loyal.
kingdoms.glyphRelationship: Glyphs are sacred signs — carved on ogham stones, used for boundaries, memorials, and druidic ritual. Each glyph is a tree, each tree is a memory.
kingdoms.collapseTelling: Not yet recorded. The Cernfolk tell their version orally. It has not been written down.

primaryLanguage.name: Gallac
primaryLanguage.description: The language of the Chieftain, the warriors, the open plains. Spoken at council fires and battle musters.
primaryLanguage.adzmystInheritance: Preserved the doubling and tripling as punctuation and ritual emphasis. Merged several glyph distinctions.
primaryLanguage.sampleGloss: The gods judge. The gods are judged.

subFamilies.name: Brythonic
subFamilies.peopleDescription: Druidic, mystical, isolationist. They keep to the islands and preserve the old ways.
subFamilies.region: Western islands, sacred groves, the misty isles
subFamilies.scriptDescription: Carved on standing stones. Used for boundaries, memorials, and sacred inscriptions.
subFamilies.relationToKingdom: Independent, but culturally aligned. They share gods, but not kings.

subFamilies.name: Goidelic
subFamilies.peopleDescription: Tribal, clan-based, poetic. The bards, the genealogists, the keepers of oral history.
subFamilies.region: Northwestern highlands, rocky coasts, clan territories
subFamilies.scriptDescription: Same script as Brythonic, different pronunciation and symbol assignments.
subFamilies.relationToKingdom: Restive, rebellious. They were conquered generations ago and still remember.
subFamilies.sampleGloss: Great is the one of the five.

kingdoms.name: Prūsland
kingdoms.region: Northeastern forests, deep lakes, amber-rich shores, vast steppes to the east
kingdoms.governmentDescription: The Elder Council chooses the Kunigs (King). The King rides with the army. The capital moves with the King — a tent-city of felt and gold.
kingdoms.peopleDescription: Fair-haired, grey-eyed, pale. Linen, fur, amber. Mysterious, reclusive, ancestral. Horse-lords of the eastern steppes.
kingdoms.glyphRelationship: Glyphs are talismans — carved in amber, worn around the neck. Each glyph is a tree, each tree is a memory. The Lakefolk believe the glyphs were spoken by the first ancestor, Prūsus, who rose from the lake.
kingdoms.collapseTelling: The Lightning and the Forest. The god Perkūnas struck the one great forest with lightning. The trees were scattered. Each tree grew alone. Each remembered only its own name. The lightning was not punishment. A forest too dense chokes itself.

primaryLanguage.name: Prūsisk
primaryLanguage.description: The language of the Kunigs, the Elder Council, the amber shrines.
primaryLanguage.adzmystInheritance: Preserved the breath-marks (doubling and tripling as punctuation). Merged 𐤎 and 𐤔. Retained the consonantal roots.
primaryLanguage.samplePhrase: Dewīs dūrai. Prūsiskas wīrst. Perkūnas perēit.
primaryLanguage.sampleGloss: The door is opening. The Prussian becomes. Perkūnas passes over.

subFamilies.name: Lietuvių
subFamilies.peopleDescription: Pagan, forest-dwelling, horse-riding. The last holdouts of the old religion.
subFamilies.region: Northern forests, deep lakes, sacred groves
subFamilies.scriptDescription: Adapted from Helorian trade contacts. Used for amber transactions and genealogies.
subFamilies.relationToKingdom: Culturally dominant. The Kunigs speaks Lithuanian at court.
subFamilies.sampleGloss: The gods give, the gods take away.

subFamilies.name: Latvji
subFamilies.peopleDescription: Seafaring, amber-trading, coastal. The merchants of Prūsia who trade with Heloria and Ealdor.
subFamilies.region: Baltic coast, amber-rich shores, fishing villages
subFamilies.scriptDescription: Same script as Lithuanian, different pronunciation and vocabulary.
subFamilies.relationToKingdom: Economically vital, politically weak. They pay taxes but are not represented in the Elder Council.

kingdoms.name: Fellmark
kingdoms.region: Northern fjords, volcanic islands, sea ice, black sands
kingdoms.governmentDescription: The High Jarl is first among equals. The Thing is the annual assembly of free men.
kingdoms.peopleDescription: Pale-skinned, light-eyed, red or blond hair. Wool, leather, iron. Seafaring, adventurous, fatalistic.
kingdoms.glyphRelationship: Glyphs are runes — carved on weapons, ships, standing stones. Cast for divination. Each rune is a fate.
kingdoms.collapseTelling: The Liberation of Tongues. The tower was built by giants who enslaved the Fellfolk. The Fellfolk broke their own tongues so the giants could not command them. The scattering was not punishment. It was liberation.

primaryLanguage.name: Nordrune
primaryLanguage.description: The language of the Jarls, the Thing, the sagas. Spoken on the sea and at the forge.
primaryLanguage.adzmystInheritance: Preserved the consonantal roots. Lost the vowel system entirely. Runes are consonants only.
primaryLanguage.sampleGloss: The fire holds. The door opens.

kingdoms.note: Fellmark is linguistically isolated due to the northern ice. No sub-families have diverged. The Fellfolk are one people, one tongue, one fate.

adzmystComparativeTable.description: How the sacred seven glyphs are rendered in each kingdom's primary language.

sharedIncantation.description: The fire holds. The door opens. — in all seven languages.
sharedIncantation.ealdor: Þæt fyr hæld. Þæt duru āþnaþ.
